description: Evaluation cases for the Kajola skill. Each case defines a prompt, required sections, and specific assertions that must hold in the generated output.

cases.name: Artisan booking marketplace (Nigeria)
cases.prompt: Design a multi-tenant artisan booking platform for Nigeria called Kajola. Artisans (barbers, tailors, mechanics) create profiles and get booked. Clients discover and pay via Paystack. Need full system architecture.

assertions.description: Phone OTP is the primary auth method
assertions.check: output contains 'phone' and 'OTP' in Section 1 or Section 2

assertions.description: Paystack is specified as payment provider
assertions.check: output contains 'Paystack' in Section 2, Section 3, and Section 8

assertions.description: SQL schema has RLS on all tables
assertions.check: every CREATE TABLE block in Section 3 has a corresponding ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statement

assertions.description: Booking conflict prevention at DB level
assertions.check: output contains 'EXCLUDE USING gist' or 'prevent_booking_conflict' trigger in Section 3

assertions.description: Offline queue included in mobile structure
assertions.check: output contains 'offline' or 'offline-queue' in Section 5

assertions.description: All env vars named and sourced
assertions.check: Section 8 contains a table with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, PAYSTACK_SECRET_KEY, TERMII_API_KEY
output contains 'HMAC' or 'webhook signature' in Section 7 or Section 4
Section 9 contains percentage values (e.g. '5%', '2.5%') and Naira amounts (e.g. '₦' or 'NGN')
Section 10 contains '10,000' or '10k' and '100,000' or '100k' and '1,000,000' or '1M'
output contains '## Assumptions Made'
Section 1 mentions at least two distinct user roles (e.g. artisan/provider and client)

cases.name: Equipment rental marketplace (East Africa)
cases.prompt: Build the complete system design for ToolHire Pro — an equipment rental marketplace for East African construction companies. Multi-tenant. M-Pesa payments. Full package.

assertions.description: M-Pesa is included as primary payment method
assertions.check: output contains 'M-Pesa' or 'Daraja' in Section 2, Section 3, and Section 4
output contains 'KES' or 'Kenya shilling' or 'multi-currency'
Section 3 contains a conflict prevention mechanism for rental slot overlaps
output contains 'tenant_id' and 'current_user_tenant_id' in Section 3
Section 3 contains tables for equipment/inventory, rentals, and availability
Section 3 or Section 4 addresses security deposit or damage tracking
Assumptions block mentions East Africa or Kenya as target market

cases.name: Food delivery platform (Ghana)
cases.prompt: Design a food delivery platform for Accra, Ghana. Restaurants list menus, customers order, riders deliver. Need multi-restaurant support, real-time order tracking, and Flutterwave payments in GHS.

assertions.check: output contains 'Flutterwave' in Sections 2, 4, and 8
output contains 'GHS' or 'Ghana cedi' or 'pesewa'
output contains 'Realtime' or 'WebSocket' or 'real-time' in Section 2 or Section 5

assertions.description: Three roles present: customer, restaurant, rider
assertions.check: Section 1 mentions three distinct personas or roles

assertions.description: Menu and order tables in schema
assertions.check: Section 3 contains tables for menus (or menu_items) and orders (or order_items)
Section 3 or Section 2 addresses rider location updates (PostGIS or coordinates)
Section 7 includes events for order state transitions (placed, accepted, picked_up, delivered)

cases.name: Minimal prompt — defaults applied correctly
cases.prompt: Design a home cleaning services marketplace.

assertions.description: All 11 sections present despite minimal prompt
assertions.check: output contains all 11 section headers
output contains 'Paystack' or 'Termii' or 'phone OTP'
output contains '## Assumptions Made' and mentions stack defaults

assertions.description: No placeholder text despite sparse input

assertions.check: Section 3 contains tables relevant to cleaning services (bookings, service_types, cleaners, or similar)

cases.name: Custom stack override respected
cases.prompt: Build a freelance marketplace for African creatives (designers, writers, developers). Use Stripe for payments and Firebase for auth instead of Supabase. Target market: pan-African.

assertions.check: output contains 'Stripe' in Sections 2, 4, and 8

assertions.description: Firebase auth used instead of Supabase Auth
assertions.check: output contains 'Firebase' in Sections 2 and 5
output mentions multiple African countries or currencies in Section 9 or Section 10
Section 3 contains tables for gigs/projects, proposals/bids, and contracts or milestones
Assumptions block mentions Firebase and Stripe as user-specified overrides

cases.name: Quality gate — zero placeholders enforcement
cases.prompt: Design a telemedicine platform for Nigeria. Doctors consult patients via video. Prescriptions managed digitally.

assertions.description: No placeholder text anywhere in output

assertions.check: output names a specific video provider (Daily.co, Twilio Video, Agora, Whereby, or similar)
Section 3 contains a prescriptions table with at minimum: id, patient_id, doctor_id, medications, issued_at
output mentions data privacy compliance (NDPR for Nigeria or equivalent) in Section 1 or Section 8
Section 3 contains booking conflict prevention for doctor appointments

assertions.description: All API endpoints have error cases
assertions.check: every endpoint block in Section 4 contains at least one 'Errors:' entry
Section 9 contains at least one specific Naira amount (₦) or percentage with a digit

scoring.notes: A case passes if 85% or more of its assertions pass. The skill passes overall if all 6 cases pass.
